Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jan-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SEAN MCHENRY, Esq. of McHenry Law Firm (Tel: 415-494-8422) for plaintiff Clint Barnes;
Atty. BRIAN DAVALOS, Esq. of Davalos Law Firm, PC (Tel: 209-400-4517) for defendants Tailors' Keep LLC and Ryan Devens.

Hearing Re: Defendants' Motion For Protective Order.

The Court notes neither side contested the court's tentative ruling.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is largely adopted. Defendant's motion for a protective order regarding dissemination of discovery is granted. The order shall be drafted based on the standard order used by the U.S. District Court of Northern California. The parties shall meet and confer on the proposed order. In the unlikely event of disputes, they are to be resolved by regular noticed motion to the Discovery Department.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
